does: This paper presents a comprehensive red teaming framework specifically designed to identify and evaluate security vulnerabilities in AI systems deployed in maritime autonomous vessels. The framework operates in two modes: proactive assessment during system design phases and reactive evaluation post-deployment. It consists of a multi-part checklist methodology that can be customized for different maritime autonomous systems and operational requirements. The authors demonstrate the framework's effectiveness by applying it to real-world maritime AI systems, where it successfully uncovers multiple previously unknown vulnerabilities and security weaknesses that could be exploited through adversarial attacks or other malicious means.
connects: This work builds on the growing field of AI adversarial robustness and security testing, which has established that AI systems face fundamental vulnerabilities from carefully crafted inputs and attacks. It extends established red teaming methodologies from cybersecurity and AI safety into the critical domain of maritime autonomous systems, where AI governs high-stakes physical operations. The paper responds to recognized gaps in practical AI security evaluation frameworks, combining insights from adversarial machine learning literature with domain-specific maritime knowledge. It addresses the inadequacy of current security examination practices for autonomous systems and incorporates lessons from both traditional cybersecurity threat modeling and emerging AI safety practices.
enables: This framework enables maritime operators and developers to systematically identify and remediate AI security vulnerabilities before deployment and throughout operational lifecycles. It provides a foundation for establishing minimum security standards and testing protocols in autonomous shipping, potentially influencing regulatory frameworks for maritime AI systems. Future work can extend this red teaming methodology to other safety-critical autonomous domains including aviation and autonomous vehicles. The framework also facilitates better threat modeling in maritime contexts, enabling more informed risk assessment and the development of robust defenses against adversarial attacks targeting navigation, obstacle detection, and collision avoidance systems in autonomous vessels.
